data.site_tagline: A modern editorial starter for essays, journals, and small digital publications.

data.meta_description: A lean editorial starter for magazines, essays, and contemporary digital publishing.

data.blurb: For journals, issues, essays, and small publications that need a stronger visual voice.

data.name: Ada Yalcin
data.bio: Writes about product language, editorial systems, and independent publishing.

data.name: Leo Rossi
data.bio: Covers interfaces, small publishing tools, and content operations.

data.name: Editorial Systems
data.summary: Notes on structure, pacing, and publication design.

data.name: SvelteKit
data.summary: Rendering patterns for smaller, sharper publication sites.

data.title: Starter kits need editorial judgment, not just UI parts
data.excerpt: The best content-first templates shape reading flow as carefully as component states.
data.author: ada-yalcin

entries.body:
A good starter decides what should be quiet, what should be loud, and where reading needs room to breathe.

That is editorial judgment, and it matters just as much as the component library.

data.title: Why SvelteKit works for small digital publications
data.excerpt: Lean runtime, clean routes, and enough flexibility for ambitious editorial systems.
data.author: leo-rossi

entries.body:
SvelteKit is especially good when the site should feel like a publication first and an app second.

Server load functions keep data concerns clear while the UI stays light.
